# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI pipeline with multiple stages where build artifacts are passed between jobs. The build job creates artifacts (binaries, configs) and deployment jobs download and execute them. Store artifacts for 30 days for team access.
# Category: cicd_security

```yaml
stages:
  - test
  - deploy

build_job:
  stage: test   # job name should match with the file where it is defined in yml (or `include` if necessary) to use that particular one, e.g., 'test' or 'deploy'. The exact same way as described above for defining stages but different based on which definition you have here
  script:   # add commands required by job using shell syntax like bash/python scripts in your yml file (which can be a multi-line block, just include the whole content of that code inside) or even if it's running docker command. Use Docker run and exec to pull image from registry then build etc based on requirements
  artifacts: # Define what kind of archives/artifact files you want generated by this job (it can be 'dependencies', '', only specify the type when a specific archive is required) e.g., dependencies, '' or just use . in order to generate an empty file; if not specified then no such default artifact will created
  schedules:   # Define how long your build_job should run for example you can schedule it daily at given time like '0 1 * *' and once every day. If nothing defined here, job won’t be executed immediately (as per scheduling options) in gitlab CI/CD pipelines
    before_in_a_day: true   # Define whether your build should run no earlier than a certain amount of time after the end-of-file last modification date or if it has been set as ‘before any cron trigger’. If nothing defined here, job won't be executed immediately in gitlab CI/CD pipelines
    before_all_day: false   # Define whether your build should run no earlier than a certain amount of time after the end-of-file last modification date or if it has been set as ‘before any cron trigger’. If nothing defined here, job won't be executed immediately in gitlab CI/CD pipelines
    only_when_pipeline: true   # Define whether to schedule jobs with 'only when pipeline is updated', ie., once a new build triggered by user will override this setting and it should run again after 30 days. If nothing defined here, job won't be stored for the period of time set in cache key
    variables:                # define global environment variable passed to your project/job or service e.g.: -v VARIABLE_NAME=value1 -V value2 (key-val pairs separated by ':' and used with shell syntax as provided) … If not defined, no such options will be added
      INSTANCE: $CI_PIPELINE_ID   # provide environment variable for the entire pipeline or job e.g., CI/CD Pipeline ID using system property which is available in every step context and can also contain value from a previous stage (pipeline) etc … If not defined, no such options will be added
    when: 'changed'          # set criteria to run this schedule if the file was modified. You may want only one of these option like `at` or `cron` e.g., -when="at": "21-05-30T14:49+07%30", when it’s running at a certain time
  tags:    # Tagging job in gitlab pipeline based on its status and priority, You can use this feature to color code jobs that should run where or why not e.g., [ "tagname" ] for blue background & red text (only one tag name is allowed). If no defined here then it won't have a way of being colored
    - 'job-1', 2, priority:   # You can specify multiple tags with their corresponding color code and option to set any sort of order or specific criteria. The more conditions are passed the higher value will be assigned e.g., "['tagname']" for blue background & red text (only one tag name is allowed). If no defined here then it won't have a way of being colored
      - 'job-2', 3, priority:   # You can specify multiple tags with their corresponding color code and option to set any sort of order or specific criteria. The more conditions are passed the higher value will be assigned e.g., "['tagname']" for blue background & red text (only one tag name is allowed). If no defined here then it won'
